schema.applies_to: which storyteller relationships this prompt fits — gate so only relevant prompts surface. Vocabulary: any | parent | grandparent | aunt_uncle (sibling | spouse | other reserved for later)
schema.prompt: question text, with tokens resolved at assembly time (see tokens below)
schema.emotional_weight: light | medium | heavy — pace sessions and gate sensitive prompts
schema.photo_friendly: true if it works attached to a photo ('tell me about this picture')
schema.follow_ups: optional probing questions; empty array if none

tokens.{address}: how the interviewer addresses this storyteller — 'Dad', 'Grandma', 'Uncle Joe'
tokens.{partner}: how the interviewer refers to the storyteller's spouse/partner — 'Mom', 'Grandpa', 'Aunt Rosa', or generic 'your wife/husband'
tokens.{asker_relation}: how the interviewer stands to the storyteller, from the storyteller's view — 'your son', 'your niece', 'your grandson'
tokens.{asker_parent}: the relative linking a grandchild/niece/nephew to the storyteller — for a grandparent, their child (the asker's parent); for an aunt/uncle, their sibling (the asker's parent)

notes: Tokens are resolved when a session is assembled, using the storyteller record (name, pronouns) and the relationship row (address term, asker_relation). The same resolved context is handed to the AI interviewer so its generated follow-ups are phrased correctly. Universal prompts use applies_to ['any']; relationship-specific ones are gated. A duplicate Spanish set keyed by lang can be seeded alongside these (English shown here).

prompts.prompt: What was your first car?
follow_ups: Where did you go in it?
What happened to it in the end?

prompts.prompt: What music did you love when you were twenty?
follow_ups: Did you ever see them live?

prompts.prompt: What did a great Saturday look like in your prime?
What's a meal that instantly brings back a memory?
What shows or movies did you never miss?
What's a hairstyle or outfit you wore that you'd never admit to now?
What's something you were really good at that might surprise me?
Where did you grow up, and what do you remember most about it?
follow_ups: What did the neighborhood sound like?

prompts.prompt: What was your childhood home like? Describe a room you can still picture.
What's the first memory you can reach back and grab?
What did you get in trouble for most often?
What were you afraid of as a kid?
Is there a smell or sound from childhood that takes you right back?
Did you have a pet? Tell me about them.
What's a place from childhood you wish you could visit one more time?
What were your own parents like to be around?
follow_ups: What did you get from each of them?

prompts.prompt: Was there a relative everyone talked about? What was their story?
What traditions did your family have that you loved — or couldn't stand?
Where did our family come from, and how did they end up where they did?
What was your relationship with your brothers and sisters like?
What do you remember about your grandparents?
What kind of student were you, honestly?
Was there a teacher who changed something for you?
What did you think you were going to be when you grew up?
Tell me about a friend from back then I've never heard about.
follow_ups: Do you know what became of them?

prompts.prompt: What's something you did as a teenager that you'd be horrified if I did?
What was a risk you took young that somehow worked out?
What was your first job, and what did it teach you?
follow_ups: What did you spend that first paycheck on?

prompts.prompt: When did you first feel like an actual adult?
What's a work moment you're proud of that no one really knows about?
Was there a path you almost took but didn't?
follow_ups: Do you ever wonder about that other life?

prompts.prompt: Who was the best boss or mentor you ever had?
What was your first place of your own like?
How did you meet {partner}?
What do you remember about your wedding day — the real stuff, not the photos?
What's something you learned about marriage the hard way?
Who was the first person you ever loved?
What does loving someone for a long time actually take?
What were you thinking the day I was born?
Is there anything you wish you'd done differently raising me?
What's something I did as a kid that you still laugh about?
What did you hope for me before you even knew who I'd become?
What was it like becoming a parent for the first time?
What do you remember about the day {asker_parent} was born?
What was {asker_parent} like as a child?
What do you remember about becoming a grandparent?
What was {asker_parent} like growing up?
What's a story about {asker_parent} they'd never tell me themselves?
Who's the best friend you ever had, and what made them that?
Was there a place where everyone knew you?
Who do you miss that's no longer around?
What's the best trip you ever took?
What's something you could do for hours and never tire of?
Tell me about something you were proud of building or fixing with your hands.
What's the most beautiful place you've ever been?
Is there a song that changes your whole mood the second it comes on?
What's the hardest thing you've ever been through?
Was there a time you weren't sure things would work out, but they did?
Who did you lean on in your worst moment?
Is there a regret you've made peace with?
What's something you believed strongly at 25 that you've completely changed your mind about?
What's the best decision you ever made? The worst?
What does a good life look like to you now?
What's something it took you most of your life to understand?
What advice would you give your 20-year-old self?
What do you want the younger people in this family to know about you?
Is there anything you've never told me that you'd want me to know?
When you're remembered, what do you hope people say?
What values do you hope get passed down through the family?
If you could leave me with one piece of advice, what would it be?
What made your life a good one?
